keyFindings.question: Q1-Q5
keyFindings.title: Eight review slices collapse into six recurring root-cause families
keyFindings.summary: The dominant clusters are MCP contract drift, memory write correctness, governed-scope/security boundaries, metadata/status drift, catalog/playbook verification drift, and deep-loop executor reliability.

keyFindings.question: Q1
keyFindings.title: MCP contract drift is divergent hand-maintenance, not one broken generator
keyFindings.summary: The public tool schema, Zod validation allow-list, handler/runtime, install guide, feature catalog, and playbook carry separate contract representations and disagree about `dryRun:false`, `mode:"apply"`, and `activeOnly`.

keyFindings.question: Q3
keyFindings.title: Entity-density cache staleness has real graph-routing impact; atomic save is crash-window risk
keyFindings.summary: The entity-density cache derives graph routing signals from title/trigger phrase terms and remains stale after memory_update until invalidation/TTL/restart; atomic save indexes before pending-file promotion, creating a narrower crash window.

keyFindings.question: Q2
keyFindings.title: Metadata drift is systemic but split across multiple owners
keyFindings.summary: Graph refresh preserves active-child chronology, `deriveStatus()` can mark placeholder summaries complete, resource maps and changelog rollups are stale catalog snapshots, and description metadata can retain stale renumbering when not regenerated.

keyFindings.question: Q2
keyFindings.title: Observed metadata blast radius includes at least four false-complete 027 draft children and multiple 026 catalog surfaces
keyFindings.summary: 027 phases 003 through 006 are draft in the phase map but complete in graph metadata, while 026 has stale resource-map, changelog rollup, parent active-child, and remediation packet progress signals.

keyFindings.question: Q4
keyFindings.title: Governed-scope defects are real, but local single-user MCP calibrates them to P1
keyFindings.summary: Community fallback bypasses the scoped pipeline and causal tools operate on bare IDs, but P0 requires a shared untrusted-principal or remote/untrusted caller model.

keyFindings.question: Q5
keyFindings.title: Fan-out code can mask failed subprocesses and serialize a single multi-lineage run
keyFindings.summary: Nonzero subprocess exits are returned as ordinary worker output, the pool counts fulfilled returns as success, and `spawnSync` blocks the async worker before additional lineages can start in the same process.

keyFindings.question: Q5
keyFindings.title: Current review summaries are suspect, but per-lineage sentinels reduce evidence of missing lineages
keyFindings.summary: Eight review summaries report one lineage while ledgers record five started and five completed entries. Individual stdout files include sentinels; the suspect artifacts are summaries and one self-invocation-refused executor audit, not necessarily the review reports themselves.

ruledOutDirections.approach: Treat each registry row as a unique root cause
ruledOutDirections.reason: Merged lineage rows duplicate near-identical findings with different wording.

ruledOutDirections.approach: Treat entity-density cache staleness as full memory corruption
ruledOutDirections.reason: The stale value affects graph-channel routing signals, not the stored memory row itself.

ruledOutDirections.approach: Treat atomic save ordering as continuous successful-save corruption
ruledOutDirections.reason: The normal path promotes the pending file after indexing; the risk is the crash/failure window between DB commit and final file promotion.

ruledOutDirections.approach: Treat metadata drift as only isolated manual edits
ruledOutDirections.reason: Graph status derivation and active-child pointer preservation are code-level mechanisms that can reproduce stale or misleading metadata.

ruledOutDirections.approach: Treat graph backfill as sufficient to repair all metadata drift
ruledOutDirections.reason: Backfill refreshes graph metadata, but active-child chronology is preserved and catalog rollups/resource maps are outside the graph metadata derivation contract.

ruledOutDirections.approach: Treat P0 as unconditional for local single-user MCP
ruledOutDirections.reason: The research charter's local trusted-client threat model lacks the untrusted shared-principal condition required for critical security severity.

ruledOutDirections.approach: Treat current review slices as proven missing-lineage artifacts
ruledOutDirections.reason: Their summaries are suspect, but captured stdout sentinels exist and ledgers show five completions per slice.
